action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13's 768-dim 8-layer MPNN with JK and virtual node achieved test AP 0.2383 (new best) after 30 epochs. Validation AP was 0.2472 and still improving, while training loss was steadily decreasing. The LR in the 30-epoch schedule had already decayed to near-zero by epoch 30, suggesting the model simply needed more epochs with a gentler annealing schedule to continue improving. Analysis: The architecture is solid and well under the 1B param limit (~31M). The highest-ROI, lowest-risk improvement is to train the same architecture for 50 epochs (67% more training) while maintaining a cosine LR schedule that is still meaningful at epoch 30 (~4e-4). Adding an EMA of weights (decay=0.999) and saving both the best validation checkpoint and its EMA version should further improve generalization. At test time, predict.py will ensemble the two available states if present. Decision: Keep model.py architecture unchanged. Update train.py to run 50 epochs with EMA and dict-style checkpointing. Update predict.py to handle the new checkpoint format and optionally ensemble multiple states. Run training now.
